item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded: Eh… it's not really thematically related to that term though? Also if I recall correctly there's already another SCP with that title or a similar reference.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded: I feel like there was a huge missed opportunity not calling this thing "The Butterfly Effect".

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Noted. It will be done.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Hm… I'm good with the Kiryu Labs link, but I'd prefer not to have the Resurrection canon link because of the way I initially conceptualized this article.

Thanks Wikiwalk!

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two proposals here. First off the mention of Dr. Kiryu can go ahead and direct the reader to the excellent Kiryu Labs Hub. Additionally, the tale Snakes and Ladders makes use of this article in a quite wonderful fashion, and has the benefit of directing readers to tales in the Resurrection canon. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Dr. Kiryu is seated in SCP-2332’s containment room, while SCP-2332 is stationed near the microphone.

To this:

Dr. Kiryu is seated in SCP-2332’s containment room, while SCP-2332 is stationed near the microphone.

Changing this:

SCP-2332: Another me will deliver it in the right time. It’s not for you. Not this side.

To this:

SCP-2332: Another me will deliver it in the right time. It’s not for you. Not this side.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was watching some old Jesse Cox playthroughs when I listened to his old theme song, and it reminded me of this article.

You could argue that SCP-408 fits this better, but this is a true space butterfly

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded: For realsies, that might be some of the best life advice I've ever seen.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
I wanna be like you Zyn when I grow up. :D

Awwwwweh. :3c

keep a log of the little things that make you smile, take care of something alive that lives with you (for me, it's eighteen small potted plants and two fish), hold a serious conversation about dumb questions with yourself once a week, and enjoy a nap from time to time. The rest is up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Kiryu: I see. One last question, how did you fly through the terrarium wall earlier?

SCP-2332: Is it strange that light can pass through glass?

Upvoted. This is a charming piece indeed.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
+1 is it odd I want a butterfly plushie that looks like this?

I like the concept of other realities existing in the same place at the same time divided. A many sided mirror is a great way to explain it too. I wanna be like you Zyn when I grow up. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: shapeshifting space butterfly is pleased

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2332: Is it strange that light can pass through glass?

Well… I GUESS NOT. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can work with that. Edited!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A set of blue laser pointers is to be available in the room at all times; all security personnel assigned to nearby areas are to carry the same laser pointers.

It would clearer to phrase it like "Each security personnel assigned to nearby areas is to carry at least one blue laser pointer at all times. A box of spare blue laser pointers is to be kept outside the containment room"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of what the butterfly says in the interview can essentially be interpreted literally, although I tried to write it as the messenger not wanting to give away too much. Gotta strike the balance between convincing this dimension's Foundation that everything is okay, without revealing information that could damage other timelines or dimensions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I particularly liked the interview. I didn't completely get all the implications of the interview, but I did like how the last line revealed everything. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zyn your butterflies are so cool.

yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Show replies to questions
hide

What is happening over there?

Business as usual.

Can the Foundation contact them?

This dimension's Foundation cannot.

Are they trying to contact our universe's Foundation?

No. The butterfly was never supposed to end up in this dimension.

What was the message they were trying to send?

Something that would possibly alter this dimension's timestream if it was passed on to the wrong Mark Kiryu.

My initial take on this was that it's inherently not that big of a deal to see these light butterflies flying around in other dimensions, but they're not supposed to exist at all in the current dimension, at least not for a hundred years or whatever. The Foundation wanted something fast (hence the light) and smart, to a degree (hence the nerve cells and the passage of words directly from one iteration of a Foundation staff member to another).

Potential spoilers
hide

This dimension's Foundation finding it was entirely a mistake. Two messenger butterflies got smashed together en route to the same place, crossed the streams, quantum entanglement, what have you. Admittedly, I'm not sure if that got through in the interview, but I wanted to leave enough room for the reader to put together their own comprehension of the little dimension hopper messenger butterfly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a fan of Fringe and Eureka, alternate universes and timelines are some of my favorite science fiction concepts. I wish you'd done a little bit more with the concept of this alternate universe.

What is happening over there? Can the Foundation contact them? Are they trying to contact our universe's Foundation? What was the message they were trying to send?

You focus entirely on the messenger itself, which is cool, but what is a messenger without a message?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zyn is female

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good, sir. I feel like I want more on this article, but I completely agree that anything else would drag it down… for those conflicting feelings alone you've earned my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All that font changing must be difficult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this mean that the butterfly is one with the pootworm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zyn.

Zyn your butterflies are so cool.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2332: Is it strange that light can pass through glass?

Here, take my first +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see!

Out of curiosity, what more do you think could've been done with the concept? I know that I do have an issue with crossing the line from subtle to too vague, and if a lot of people aren't getting the subtext without the discussion, I'm all for making edits so more people can enjoy it after the first reading.

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
This is what would’ve been my SCP-2000 contest entry

I'm surprised, because this reminded me of nothing so much as the raspberry. Now I'm kind of sad they aren't related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone thinks that something that travels as fast as light needs to look like other things that are fast. In another time, you think differently.

Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understood it AFTER you explained it in the discussion. However, I feel like you did not do enough with that concept. I won't down vote primarily because I like the concept, but I won't up vote because I dislike the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making me fall in love again, zyn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved it, but it seemed kind of odd when it had an interview. You might want to mention in the description that it can speak, as I don't believe there was anything about that in there.
Actually, I just read a little closer, and it turns out that I'm an idiot, I saw the speech bit now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As stated previously in chat, I'm close to an upvote on this one. I enjoyed the interview, which is a neat trick because interviews are difficult to write successfully, both from the proper execution and article enhancement angles. As a whole, there's a pleasant degree of puzzlement that I get from this article; not enough is given away to make things immediately apparent, but it's not so veiled as to be meaningless nonsense.

That said, I'm left with the feeling that I've read three-quarters of a good novel, and it's stopped before I've found anything out. That's okay sometimes, since mystery is a frequently employed tool around here that I enjoy very much, but this needs just a little bit more to get it over the finish line. Not much more, because I don't want this clearly explained and in a sense disenchanted, but maybe just a little more suggestion. I mean, what kind of message is this? Hints of that nature could add an extra layer to this article in terms of the background tone. As it stands, I'm in high neutral-vote territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can only read the butterfly dialog in the voice of the pkunk from star control 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A lot of people in the draft stage thought that adding more would bog down the article (one person thought the description as it is was too long), but I can understand your view. This is shorter compared to my other articles.

It is a transdimensional butterfly, which is neat, but I felt that the execution was missing a certain charm.

It's not just that, per se. Did you get the implications of how the butterfly was created and by whom?

item.title: Re: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2332: Is it strange that light can pass through glass?" And with the conclusion of that sentence +1
I was pulled in from the start ,but when I read that last sentence it hit me on how differently it sees and interacts with things(I also found the sentence a clever mind boggler). Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know.
The concept sounds extremely intriguing, but the article itself ends before you really do anything with it and I felt that it lacked a suitable payoff.
The nebula easter egg is cute, but I felt that the article really lacked a hook.
It is a transdimensional butterfly, which is neat, but I felt that the execution was missing a certain charm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely killer interview. Totally digging it. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great job, Zyn! :D Definitely gonna draw this one up; love how you implement all the little tidbits of information in your articles! (^_^)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you did there, with the nebula and angel…

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
You had one job, butterfly.

This is what would’ve been my SCP-2000 contest entry, had I gotten past the brainstorming/scrawl notes in my notebook stage.

Show spoilers
hide spoilers

Basically, the Foundation in some other time/space/whatever managed to manufacture a means to send messages between parallel dimensions. The messengers are these butterflies made of light and human brain cells, who have enough embedded intelligence to communicate as well as relay the messages they were created to store.

Mark Kiryu in one dimension (where these butterfly messengers are commonplace) was trying to send a message to Mark Kiryu in another dimension but some funky space stuff happened and another butterfly smacked into it. Basically, two of them en route to the same message delivery spot got mushed up by accident and ended up in the wrong time/space/dimension. The resulting butterfly is basically waiting for the parallel universe it came from to right itself so it can go home.

Different kinds of light are like food to these butterflies. You are what you eat. d: This one happens to like ultraviolet light while blue light makes it feel nauseous.

Many thanks to all these nice people who read this over in chat! /deep breath ProcyonLotor, Riemann, Gaffsey, MisterFlames, Zolgamax, Eskobear, Devereaux, Gamerkid2345, TheTyrantis, TroyL, TwistedGears, Athena_Grey, Kalinin, Ihp(kmn) Sorry if I forgot anyone.

Some easy easter eggs, look up the NGC 6302 nebula and the etymology of the word “angel”. ;)

Edit: Many thanks to the super awesome Dr Lonewolf, who made the new pictures!

Here's the attribution information (again, thank you Dr Lonewolf) for the source images; all are CC-compliant and allow for modification in their license:

Show attribution info
hide
